incidents.victim: Vimeo (and other Anodot customers including Rockstar Games, McGraw Hill, ADT)
incidents.vector: Third-party supply-chain breach: attackers obtained authentication tokens from analytics vendor Anodot and used them to access cloud environments of multiple downstream customers without breaching them directly.
incidents.impact: Vimeo confirmed user and customer data was stolen, including technical data, video titles and metadata, and some customer email addresses; no video content, logins, or payment data were accessed. The same Anodot compromise has been tied to data theft at Rockstar Games, McGraw Hill, and ADT, with the attackers threatening leaks unless ransom is paid.

incidents.vector: Multi-stage social engineering: attackers posed as legal or VC contacts via Calendly and compromised Telegram accounts, then redirected victims to typo-squatted Zoom URLs. The fake Zoom lobby used stolen webcam footage from prior victims and AI-generated avatars, then used a ClickFix 'SDK update' prompt to install malware.
incidents.impact: From initial click to full system compromise — including credential theft, crypto wallet theft, Telegram session theft, and persistent C2 — typically completed in under five minutes; attackers maintained access for up to 66 days in one investigated case. Stolen webcam footage feeds a self-reinforcing deepfake pipeline used to lure additional victims.

incidents.vector: Email-bombing the target inbox, then contacting the user via Microsoft Teams from an external account posing as IT help desk. Victims are walked through installing a fake 'Mailbox Repair Utility' that drops a malicious browser extension (SnowBelt) and follow-on tooling (SnowGlaze, SnowBasin, AutoHotkey scripts, portable Python).
incidents.impact: SnowBelt acts as a backdoor that maintains access to corporate accounts and enables lateral movement without re-authentication. The credential-harvesting page deliberately rejects the first two password submissions to capture credentials twice.

incidents.victim: Julia Klöckner, President of the German Bundestag (and a sensitive group chat including Chancellor Friedrich Merz)
incidents.vector: Phishing campaign targeting the Signal messaging accounts of senior German officials.
incidents.impact: Klöckner's Signal account was compromised, exposing a sensitive group chat that included Chancellor Merz; Merz's own account was not compromised. German officials say the data leak has been stopped but cannot rule out other affected individuals. Part of a broader Russian campaign against Signal and WhatsApp accounts of European decision-makers.

incidents.victim: US Federal Civilian Executive Branch agencies and other Cisco ASA / Firepower customers
incidents.vector: APT actor exploited n-day vulnerabilities CVE-2025-20333 and CVE-2025-20362 in Cisco ASA / FTD WebVPN to deploy a custom backdoor (FIRESTARTER) on Cisco Firepower and Secure Firewall devices, abusing the Cisco Service Platform mount list to persist across reboots and even patching.
incidents.impact: FIRESTARTER allows remote arbitrary code execution inside the LINA process and survives firmware patching, meaning previously-patched devices may still be compromised. CISA has updated Emergency Directive 25-03 requiring federal agencies to identify and mitigate compromised devices; co-sealed advisories from NCSC-UK and ACSC indicate global impact. Linked to the same actor (UAT-4356, behind the 2024 ArcaneDoor campaign) that Cisco Talos has tracked since 2024.

incidents.vector: Data was originally stolen from Hallmark's Salesforce environment during a 2024 hacking wave; the same criminal extortion group leaked the records publicly after Hallmark refused to pay a ransom.
incidents.impact: Personal information of 1.7 million Hallmark customers was leaked on a criminal data-leak site. Part of a broader wave of Salesforce-related extortion incidents tied to the same group.

incidents.vector: Hackers breached the finance ministry's computer systems and diverted funds during a foreign debt repayment process.
incidents.impact: Approximately $2.5 million was stolen. Funds had been intended for debt repayment to Australia, and Sri Lanka has asked Australian law enforcement for help. A separate $600,000 payment to the US Postal Service has also gone missing, raising broader concerns about government financial controls as the country recovers from default.

incidents.vector: Initial intrusion linked to malware disguised as Roblox cheat tooling that compromised a developer/operator endpoint, giving the criminal extortion group access to Vercel's environment.
incidents.impact: Vercel confirmed customer data was stolen and is being sold online; the company notified affected customers and recommended rotating keys and secrets. Fallout has expanded to additional Vercel customers and third-party systems, and the same criminal group claimed credit publicly.

incidents.vector: Cyberattack of unspecified initial access vector caused Tallahassee to shut down its IT network; surrounding Leon County disconnected its IT network from the city to prevent contamination — language typically associated with ransomware.
incidents.impact: Multiple city websites and public services went offline. Recovery is ongoing.

incidents.vector: Attackers exploited a vulnerability in the Kelp DAO platform to withdraw funds as rsETH (restaked Ether) tokens.
incidents.impact: Approximately $292 million in crypto assets stolen — the largest known crypto heist of 2026 to date.

incidents.vector: Attackers exploited vulnerabilities in the protocol's margin trading feature, having pre-positioned by creating fake token pools through which operations were routed.
Security breach disclosed by Booking.com; specific intrusion vector not publicly stated.
incidents.impact: Customer personal data exposed, including booking details, names, emails, home addresses, and phone numbers. No financial data accessed; Booking.com reset reservation PINs for affected customers.

incidents.vector: Sophisticated distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attack against BlueSky's infrastructure that intensified throughout the day.
incidents.impact: BlueSky operated intermittently for almost a day, with a prolonged outage. Additional DDoS attempts continued throughout the week but failed to bring the platform down again.

incidents.victim: Operators of Rockwell Automation / Allen-Bradley industrial control systems (worldwide, ~5,600 exposed IPs)
incidents.vector: Iran-aligned threat cluster (publicly known as Cyber Av3ngers) installed Rockwell Automation FactoryTalk software on attacker-controlled VPS infrastructure and used it to scan for and exploit exposed Rockwell PLCs and FactoryTalk services, expanding beyond their prior focus on Unitronics PLCs.
incidents.impact: Targeting of operational technology / industrial control systems in critical sectors including water utilities. CISA issued an advisory on Apr 7 mirroring Unit 42's findings; activity coincides with the broader Iran/US/Israel conflict and heightened risk to OT environments.

incidents.victim: Ukrainian prosecutors (170+) and the militaries of Greece, Romania, and Serbia
incidents.vector: Email-account compromise campaign attributed to a unit of Russia's GRU military intelligence agency.
incidents.impact: Russian operators read the email of more than 170 Ukrainian prosecutors to gain access to investigative information, and breached military email accounts in Greece, Romania, and Serbia in the same campaign.

incidents.victim: LiteLLM (97M monthly downloads), Checkmarx (KICS, GitHub Actions), Aqua Trivy users — and downstream organizations consuming those packages
incidents.vector: A group calling itself TeamPCP used valid stolen credentials (most likely lifted from prior infostealer infections) to gain write access to maintainer repositories. They injected credential-harvesting payloads into LiteLLM and poisoned Checkmarx GitHub Actions workflows; a follow-on wave compromised the KICS Docker image, GitHub Action, and VS Code extensions for a second time.
incidents.impact: Malware silently exfiltrated cloud access keys, secrets, AI API keys, and CI/CD credentials from every install — including the AI keys LiteLLM was designed to manage. The campaign cascaded across at least five software ecosystems in a week, with TeamPCP claiming over 300 GB of stolen data and operating as a ransomware/extortion affiliate. Bitwarden CLI is among the confirmed downstream victims.

incidents.victim: Retail and hospitality companies (also healthcare, technology, transportation, logistics; specific victims undisclosed)
incidents.vector: Voice-phishing (vishing) attacks impersonating IT support, plus phishing pages mimicking corporate single sign-on, used to steal credentials from employees. Attackers scrape internal directories to target executives and pivot into senior accounts, then access SaaS, Microsoft Graph, Salesforce APIs, internal repositories, and SharePoint.
incidents.impact: Group (associated with The Com cybercrime community) makes seven-figure ransom demands and operates a data-leak site to pressure non-payers. RH-ISAC reports some operators have escalated to swatting executives to increase pressure.

incidents.victim: Swedish thermal power plant in western Sweden
incidents.vector: Pro-Russian hacktivist group with connections to Russia's intelligence and security services attempted a destructive attack on the plant's industrial environment.
incidents.impact: The plant's built-in safeguards detected and stopped the intrusion before any operational disruption. Sweden's minister of civil defense disclosed the incident in April 2026 and publicly attributed it to Russia.
